political.s: Per verified FEC data, Wendy's PAC leans right with $93K raised and approximately 70% of donations directed to Republican candidates. OpenSecrets confirms the PAC gave $104,500 to federal candidates in the 2023–2024 cycle, with total contributions of $172,358, and reported zero federal lobbying spend. Historically, Wendy's PAC has directed the majority of its contributions to Republican congressional candidates.

charity.s: Wendy's has a decades-long relationship with the Dave Thomas Foundation for Adoption, raising over $343 million cumulatively since its founding. In 2024, Wendy's delivered a record-breaking $23 million through in-restaurant campaigns for the Foundation. The Dave Thomas Foundation for Adoption holds a 4-star Charity Navigator rating, and 15,500+ children have found permanent homes through the Wendy's Wonderful Kids program.

environment.s: Wendy's reduced Scope 1 and 2 emissions by 37% from its 2019 baseline as of 2024, and increased franchisee enrollment in its Energy Challenge by over 50% year-over-year. The company achieved 76% sustainably sourced customer-facing packaging and committed to no deforestation across at-risk commodities by 2025. However, Scope 3 supply chain emissions—particularly from beef production—remain a significant ongoing challenge given the company's core fresh-beef identity.

labor.s: Wendy's Glassdoor rating is 3.4 out of 5 stars across 14,000+ reviews, with compensation rated just 2.8/5 and only 51% of workers recommending the company. Median worker compensation was $28,675 in 2024, roughly $14.69/hour, and Wendy's does not set a company-wide minimum wage above state requirements for franchise locations. An NLRB case in 2015 involved a settled unfair labor practice charge over handbook rules, and worker complaints around low pay, management quality, and inconsistent scheduling are common.

dei.s: Wendy's has maintained a Chief DEI Officer role since 2021, though the title was quietly rebranded to 'Global Chief Culture and Inclusion Officer' amid broader industry DEI rollbacks in 2025. The company scored 100 on the HRC Corporate Equality Index in prior years and offers multiple employee resource groups. Glassdoor DEI rating sits at 3.6/5, in line with the restaurant industry average, and McKinsey's Women in the Workplace assessment showed Wendy's meeting 100% of recommended DEI actions by 2022.

animals.s: Wendy's is a major meat-serving QSR and sources beef, chicken, and pork for consumption—animal testing for cosmetics is not applicable. The company maintains an Animal Welfare Council founded in 2001 with guidance from Dr. Temple Grandin, and conducts annual internal and third-party supplier audits. Wendy's has set a 2030 goal to source 100% of beef, chicken, and pork from suppliers prohibiting routine use of medically important antibiotics, and transitioned pork supply away from gestation stalls in 2022.

guns.s: Wendy's is a quick-service restaurant chain and does not sell or manufacture firearms. No company policy regarding in-store firearms carry has been prominently disclosed or publicized. This category is not applicable to Wendy's core business operations.

privacy.s: Wendy's corporate privacy policy covers its website, mobile app, and company-operated restaurant data, but explicitly excludes franchisee-operated locations from its policy scope—a notable gap given most restaurants are franchise-operated. The policy includes CCPA rights for California consumers including opt-out of data sale and deletion requests. No major independent privacy ratings (EFF, Mozilla) are available for Wendy's, and the privacy policy at-a-glance document was last updated in 2019, suggesting limited recent transparency improvements.

execPay.s: CEO Kirk Tanner was paid approximately $17.3 million in 2024, while the median Wendy's worker earned $28,675—a CEO-to-worker pay ratio of approximately 604:1, one of the highest among major restaurant chains. Shareholders have approved executive compensation with over 95% 'say-on-pay' support for eight consecutive years. The significant disparity, particularly against a median wage of roughly $14.69/hour, raises concerns about income equity within the organization.

fec.committeeName: THE WENDYS COMPANY POLITICAL ACTION COMMITTEE
